Metadata.title: GraphiCode — Engineer. Visualize. Understand.
Metadata.description: Interactive LabVIEW learning with real execution visualization, video lessons, and practical simulations.

tips: In LabVIEW, a node only fires once data has arrived on every one of its input wires — that's dataflow.
Wires carry data between nodes; their thickness and color tell you the data type at a glance.
A While Loop keeps running until its conditional terminal says stop — much like a do/while loop.
Two parallel wires with no data dependency can execute at the same time, automatically.
A broken run arrow means LabVIEW caught a wiring error before you ever ran the VI.
Shift registers carry a value from one loop iteration into the next.
A For Loop's count terminal (N) decides how many times the code inside repeats.
Every VI has a front panel for the interface and a block diagram for the wired logic.

Landing.heroSubtitle: The interactive LabVIEW platform that animates every line of dataflow — so you don't just read about execution, you see it happen.
Landing.whatIntro: LabVIEW is a graphical programming language built by NI for engineers and scientists. Instead of writing lines of text, you wire together visual blocks — and data flows along those wires to drive the measurement, test, and automation systems used across industry and research.

whatCards.title: Graphical dataflow
whatCards.body: No syntax to memorize. You connect nodes with wires, and each node runs the moment its data arrives — logic becomes a picture you can follow.

whatCards.title: Virtual Instruments (VIs)
whatCards.body: Every LabVIEW program is a VI with two faces: a Front Panel for controls and displays, and a Block Diagram that holds the graphical code.

whatCards.title: Built for the real world
whatCards.body: From lab measurement and signal processing to robotics and industrial automation, LabVIEW powers the hardware engineers rely on every day.

Landing.featuresTitle: Everything you need to master LabVIEW

features.title: Video Lessons
features.body: Instructor-led video walkthroughs of every LabVIEW concept — from basic controls to advanced SubVI architecture.

features.title: Animated Execution
features.body: Watch data flow through block diagrams — nodes glow, dots travel along wires, every step is visible.

features.title: Interactive Quizzes
features.body: Multiple-choice, drag-and-drop, and output-prediction challenges integrated into each lesson.

features.title: Progress Tracking
features.body: Personalized dashboards — completed lessons, quiz scores, learning streaks, and next recommendations.

Landing.learnTitle: What you'll be able to do
Landing.learnIntro: A guided path from your very first wire to modular, real-world VIs.
outcomes: Read and build block diagrams confidently, wiring data from input to output.
Control execution with For loops, While loops, and Case structures.
Handle real measurement data using arrays, clusters, and waveforms.
Hold state and respond to input with shift registers and event structures.
Break programs into reusable SubVIs for clean, modular design.
Trace and debug execution visually — predict outputs before you run.
Landing.demoTitle: See a VI run, step by step
Landing.ctaSubtitle: Create your free account and start learning with animated execution visualization.
Landing.footerTagline: Interactive LabVIEW Learning Platform · All rights reserved
